name: Maurice Jarre
abstract: Maurice-Alexis Jarre (French pronunciation: [mɔʁis alɛksi ʒaʁ]; 13 September 1924 – 28 March 2009) was a French composer and conductor, mainly of film scores. He was particularly known for his collaborations with film director David Lean, composing the scores to all of his films from 1962 to 1984.

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded for Maurice Jarre, who did Dr. Zhivago and all those films.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012
